message:
chore(wasm): narrow unused-at-target imports to silence wasm32 warnings

Two surgical import-narrowing changes so `cargo check
--target=wasm32-unknown-unknown` stops warning about unused
imports on the wasm path.

1. crates/ftui-showcase-wasm/src/runner_core.rs: the three
   ftui_layout types used only from the test module —
   PaneDockPreview, PaneDockZone, and PaneResizeGrip — are moved
   off the module-top `use` statement and into the
   `#[cfg(test)] mod tests { … }` block's own `use ftui_layout::{
   PaneDockPreview, PaneDockZone, PaneResizeGrip };` import. The
   remaining module-top `use` now lists only the types referenced
   by the non-test code (PANE_EDGE_GRIP_INSET_CELLS,
   PANE_MAGNETIC_FIELD_CELLS, PaneDragResizeEffect, PaneId, etc.)
   so wasm target builds — which compile the non-test surface
   only — no longer import unused symbols.

2. crates/ftui-demo-showcase/src/screens/explainability_cockpit.rs:
   the VecDeque import, std::fs, and std::io::{self, BufRead}
   imports are only referenced from the file-IO helpers that are
   unavailable / meaningless on wasm32 (the cockpit reads
   explainability trace files from disk on native, and on wasm
   the trace source is different). Wrap each in
   #[cfg(not(target_arch = "wasm32"))] so wasm builds stop
   compiling them — matching the existing cfg gates further down
   the file. HashMap / PathBuf / SystemTime / StdCell stay
   unconditional because they're used in the shared wasm+native
   code paths.

No behaviour change on native. On wasm: the `use` warnings go
away and the resulting binary is unchanged.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
